mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion, reducing recession risk and supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when PAYEMS growth remains positive.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134041.0 thousand as of March 2011 indicates ongoing post-crisis employment recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with positive labor momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth months produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the Fed signals commitment to monetary tightening. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premiums expand in response to higher discount rates and reduced liquidity.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.62% as of March 2011, already above 3.5% threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising policy rates
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in < 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, this interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days, as synchronized demand expansion reduces recession probability and supports corporate profit margins.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both show positive MoM growth in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2009.0 thousand and RSAFS at 351461.0 million as of February 2011
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in post-crisis recovery
failure_condition: Co-growth instances produce positive 20-day returns in < 52% of cases, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month for 2+ consecutive months, signaling supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during late-cycle contractions, as manufacturing weakness precedes broader economic slowdown and earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines MoM for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.016 index points as of March 2011
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging supply-side weakness
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 60-day returns in < 48% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, creating a negative real rate environment. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as financial repression benefits equity holders relative to bond holders, driving capital rotation into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 198.5 index and FEDFUNDS at 3.62% as of March 2011
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Co-triggered instances produce positive 20-day returns in < 53% of cases, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, signaling labor market deterioration and increased recession risk. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to weakening labor demand.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.1% as of March 2011, indicating ongoing post-crisis labor market normalization
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase within recovery cycle
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce negative 5-day returns in < 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive and above 2% annualized rate, indicating sustained economic expansion. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as expanding economic activity supports corporate revenue growth and profit margins across the business cycle.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11089.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q4 2010, indicating post-crisis recovery trajectory
regime_hint: any regime with positive real GDP growth
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth periods produce positive 60-day returns in < 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized supply and labor contraction. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during demand-shock regimes, as joint weakness indicates broad-based economic deterioration and earnings compression.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and PAYEMS both decline MoM in same month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 118.016 and PAYEMS at 134041.0 as of March 2011
regime_hint: demand shock or recession onset with synchronized supply-labor contraction
failure_condition: Co-decline instances produce negative 20-day returns in < 52% of cases, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year windows.
